summary: Final verification of GDPR password_resets cascade fix (iteration_7 bug). Confirmed server.py:2162 now uses `await db.password_resets.delete_many({'user_id': user_id})` (matching the schema inserted by forgot_password() at server.py:635-639). Re-ran the entire v8 admin-console suite — 41/41 PASS, including TestGdprDeleteCascade::test_delete_user_wipes_password_resets which previously failed in iteration_7. The cascade test now confirms: (1) create user, (2) POST /auth/password/forgot returns reset_token_dev_only, (3) DELETE /admin/users/{uid} returns 200, (4) GET /admin/users/{uid} returns 404, (5) POST /auth/password/reset with the stale token returns 400 (stale token successfully invalidated by the cascade). No regressions in the other 40 tests (admin auth guards, /admin/connectors/stats, /admin/devices/stats, /admin/engagement, /admin/health all 5 checks, /admin/users/{uid} detail, /admin/users/{uid} delete self-block + non-admin cascade, regression smoke for /auth/me, /admin/stats, /admin/users, /admin/audit, /admin/billing/health, /metrics/summary, /connectors, /watches).

minor.issue: Response body at server.py:2166 still returns `deleted_email` — minor PII echo. INFO log was cleaned (line 2165 logs only user_id) but the API response body still contains the email. Carried over from iteration_7; not retested as scope was the cascade bug only.

action_items: OPTIONAL (carried from iter7): drop `deleted_email` from the admin_delete_user response body (server.py:2166) for full PII consistency.
OPTIONAL (carried from iter6/7): refactor server.py (now 2222 lines) — admin endpoints should move to routers/admin.py.
OPTIONAL (carried from iter7): define a single INDEX_SPEC module-level dict and use it in both on_startup() and admin_health() to remove duplication.

OPTIONAL: add 'password_resets' to the existing `collections_to_clean` list rather than keeping a separate explicit line — already iterates with {'user_id': user_id} and would unify the cleanup loop (DRY).
critical_code_review_comments: Fix is correct and minimal — uses {'user_id': user_id} matching the actual stored schema. The explanatory comment at line 2161 is clear.

Defensive suggestion: collapse the special-case password_resets delete into the collections_to_clean loop. Lines 2152-2162 would become a single loop with one extra string in the list — eliminates the risk of a future contributor adding another user-keyed collection in only one of the two places.

Still no automated guardrail test that enumerates every user-keyed collection and asserts admin_delete_user empties them all (recommended in iter6 and iter7 reviews). Without it, the next user-scoped collection added is at risk of this same bug pattern.

success_rate.backend: 100% (41/41) — GDPR cascade bug fully resolved, all admin-console regressions pass

seed_data_creation: Per-test TEST_v8_<uuid>@example.com users created and cleaned up by tests. Cascade test additionally creates a password_resets row that is now correctly wiped by admin delete.
context_for_next_testing_agent: BASE_URL=https://b5756d38-6d5c-4694-ae0a-e29e724ded9b.preview.emergentagent.com. v8 admin-console suite (/app/backend/tests/test_healthbridge_v8_admin_console.py) is the canonical regression suite — 41 tests, ~12s runtime, all green. The GDPR cascade bug across iter6→iter7→iter8 is fully resolved at server.py:2162. forgot endpoint = /api/auth/password/forgot; reset = /api/auth/password/reset; dev token returned as `reset_token_dev_only`. Admin password is in /app/memory/test_credentials.md.
rca_of_issue: BUG (iter7): DELETE /api/admin/users/{user_id} failed to wipe password_resets because the delete filter used {'email': target['email']} while forgot_password() at server.py:635-639 inserts documents with key `user_id` and no email field. FIX (iter8 verified): server.py:2162 now does `await db.password_resets.delete_many({'user_id': user_id})`. VERIFICATION (this iteration): TestGdprDeleteCascade::test_delete_user_wipes_password_resets now passes — after admin delete, posting the stale reset token to /auth/password/reset returns 400 (Invalid or expired reset token), confirming the password_resets row was actually removed from MongoDB. Suite total: 41/41 green; no regressions.
